THOUGHT:
The current reference list effectively covers ML system debt, out-of-distribution generalization, and uncertainty quantification, but lacks a foundational citation addressing **optimizer convergence failures and training instability**, a major real-world pitfall in deep learning (Category 3). I will search for the seminal ICLR 2018 paper that empirically demonstrates Adam's failure to converge on standard convex and non-convex problems and introduces AMSGrad as a corrective method. This paper is critical for discussing how widely adopted training algorithms can silently fail, causing wasted compute, poor generalization, and delayed deployments.
